asciichat_error_t options_init (int argc, char **
  argv)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/options/options.h>


Initialize options by parsing command-line arguments with unified
    mode detection.


Parameters


argc Argument count from main()


argv Argument vector from main()


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_USAGE on parse
  errors


Main Entry Point for the options system. This function:





  
1.

  
Mode Detection: Analyzes argv to detect requested mode:

  
 •

  
Looks for mode keywords: "server", "client",
      "mirror", "acds", etc.

  
 •

  
Checks for session string pattern (word-word-word) → client
    mode

  
 •

  
Falls back to showing help if no mode specified

  
2.

  
Binary-Level Parsing: Processes global options before mode
      detection:

  
 •

  
--help / -h → prints help and exits

  
 •

  
--version / -v → prints version and exits

  
 •

  
--verbose / -V → sets verbose level (stackable)

  
 •

  
--quiet / -q → sets quiet mode

  
 •

  
--log-file / -L → redirects logs to file

  
 •

  
--log-level → sets log verbosity level

  
3.

  
Mode-Specific Parsing: Uses mode-specific parser for detected
    mode:

  
 •

  
Parses mode-specific options (server, client, mirror, acds)

  
 •

  
Each mode has different set of supported options

  
 •

  
Options are validated with mode bitmasks

  
4.

  
Configuration File Loading (if --config specified):

  
 •

  
Loads TOML config file

  
 •

  
Merges config file settings with command-line options

  
 •

  
Command-line takes precedence over config file

  
5.

  
Validation and Defaults:

  
 •

  
Applies default values to unspecified options

  
 •

  
Validates numeric ranges, file existence, formats

  
 •

  
Performs cross-field validation (dependencies, conflicts)

  
 •

  
Checks mode-specific constraints

  
6.

  
RCU Publishing:

  
 •

  
Initializes RCU state if not already done

  
 •

  
Publishes options struct for lock-free thread-safe access

  
 •

  
After this point, GET_OPTION() and options_get()
      are valid




Detected Mode Storage: The detected mode is stored in
    options_t->detected_mode and accessible via:




asciichat_mode_t mode = GET_OPTION(detected_mode);



Mode Detection Examples:




# Mode keywords (explicit)
./ascii-chat server --port 8080        # MODE_SERVER
./ascii-chat client example.com:9000   # MODE_CLIENT
./ascii-chat mirror --color            # MODE_MIRROR
./ascii-chat acds --port 27225         # MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE
# Session strings (implicit client mode)
./ascii-chat word-word-word            # MODE_CLIENT (session string)
# Help/version (special handling)
./ascii-chat --help                    # Shows help, may exit(0)
./ascii-chat --version                 # Shows version, may exit(0)



Return Values:


    
  
  • ASCIICHAT_OK: Parsing succeeded normally
    • 
  
  • ERROR_USAGE: Parse error occurred (usage info printed to
      stderr)
    • 
  
  • Other error codes: Unexpected errors during initialization
    • 



Special Handling for --help and --version: These flags are
    handled specially and may cause early exit via exit(0). The function will
    still return ASCIICHAT_OK in these cases for compatibility.




Environment Variables: Some options can fall back to
    environment variables:


    
  
  • WEBCAM_DISABLED: Enable test pattern when set to "1",
      "true", etc.
    • 
  
  • ASCII_CHAT_KEY_PASSWORD: Fallback for encrypted key passphrases
    • 
  
  • SSH_AUTH_SOCK: For SSH agent key access
    • 



Typical Usage:




int main(int argc, char **argv) {


    asciichat_error_t err = options_init(argc, argv);


    if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


        // Could be ERROR_USAGE (invalid options) or other errors


        // usage() already printed to stderr for ERROR_USAGE


        return 1;


    }


    // Options now available via GET_OPTION() and options_get()


    asciichat_mode_t mode = GET_OPTION(detected_mode);


    const char *addr = GET_OPTION(address);


    // ... application code ...


    return 0;
}



Note


Must be called once at program startup before accessing
  options



Should be called before creating worker threads




Global option variables initialized by this function




Returns ERROR_USAGE for invalid options (usage already
  printed)




--help and --version may exit directly via exit(0)




After return, options accessible via GET_OPTION() macro




Initialize all command-line options from argv and environment
    variables




Main entry point for the options parsing system. This
    function:


    
  
  • Detects the mode from the first positional argument (server, client,
      mirror, etc.)
    • 
  
  • Parses binary-level options (--help, --version, --log-file, etc.)
    • 
  
  • Parses mode-specific options
    • 
  
  • Validates cross-field option dependencies
    • 
  
  • Initializes defaults from OPT_*_DEFAULT defines
    • 
  
  • Publishes options via RCU for thread-safe lock-free access
    • 



Option Processing Order:



  
1.

  
Binary-level options (parsed from all args regardless of mode)

  
2.

  
Mode detection (from first positional argument)

  
3.

  
Mode-specific options (from remaining args filtered by mode)

  
4.

  
Defaults initialization (for unspecified options)

  
5.

  
Cross-field validation (dependencies between options)

  
6.

  
RCU publication (make available to all threads)




Return Values:


    
  
  • ASCIICHAT_OK - Initialization successful
    • 
  
  • ERROR_USAGE - Invalid usage (help/usage already printed to stdout)
    • 
  
  • Other errors - Check errno context via HAS_ERRNO()
    • 



Special Behavior:


    
  
  • --help causes help text to print and exit(0) (never returns)
    • 
  
  • --version causes version to print and exit(0) (never returns)
    • 



Parameters


argc Argument count from main()


argv Argument vector from main()


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_USAGE on parse error,
  other on fatal error


Note


Global option variables initialized by this function



Returns ERROR_USAGE for invalid options (usage already
  printed)




--help and --version may exit directly via exit(0)




After return, options accessible via GET_OPTION() macro




Definition at line 856 of file
    lib/options/options.c.


856                                                       {
857   log_debug("options_init: starting with argc=%d, argv[0]=%s, argv[1]=%s", argc, argv[0], argc > 1 ? argv[1] : "N/A");
858   // NOTE: --grep filter is initialized in main.c BEFORE any logging starts
859   // This allows ALL logs (including from shared_init) to be filtered
860   // Validate arguments (safety check for tests)
861   if (argc < 0 || argc > 128) {
862     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid argc: %d", argc);
863   }
864   if (argv == NULL) {
865     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "argv is NULL");
866   }
867   // Validate all argv elements are non-NULL up to argc
868   for (int i = 0; i < argc; i++) {
869     if (argv[i] == NULL) {
870       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "argv[%d] is NULL (argc=%d)", i, argc);
871     }
872   }
873   // Initialize RCU options system (must be done before any threads start)
874   // This must happen FIRST, before any other initialization
875   asciichat_error_t rcu_init_result = options_state_init();
876   if (rcu_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
877     return rcu_init_result;
878   }
879 
880   // ========================================================================
881   // STAGE 1: Mode Detection and Binary-Level Option Handling
882   // ========================================================================
883 
884   // Check for binary-level actions FIRST (before mode detection)
885   // These actions may take arguments, so we need to check them before mode detection
886   bool show_version = false;
887   bool create_config = false;
888   bool create_manpage = false;
889   bool has_action = false; // Track if any action flag is present
890   const char *config_create_path = NULL;
891   const char *manpage_create_path = NULL;
892 
893   // ========================================================================
894   // STAGE 1A: Quick scan for action flags FIRST (they bypass mode detection)
895   // ========================================================================
896   // Quick scan for action flags (they may have arguments)
897   // This must happen BEFORE logging initialization so we can suppress logs before shared_init()
898   // Also scan for --quiet / -q so we can suppress logging from the start
899   // Also scan for --color early so it affects help output colors
900   bool user_quiet = false;
901   int parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_AUTO; // Store parsed color value until opts is created
902   bool color_setting_found = false;
903   bool check_update_flag_seen = false;
904   bool no_check_update_flag_seen = false;
905 
906   // FIRST: Scan entire argv for --help (special case - works before OR after mode)
907   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
908     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-h") == 0) {
909       // Scan backwards to find if a mode was specified before --help
910       asciichat_mode_t help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY; // Default to discovery mode (binary-level help)
911       for (int j = i - 1; j >= 1; j--) {
912         if (argv[j][0] != '-') {
913           if (strcmp(argv[j], "server") == 0) {
914             help_mode = MODE_SERVER;
915           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "client") == 0) {
916             help_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
917           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "mirror") == 0) {
918             help_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
919           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "discovery-service") == 0) {
920             help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
921           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "discovery") == 0) {
922             help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
923           }
924           break; // Found first non-flag argument
925         }
926       }
927 
928       // Show help for the detected mode (or binary-level if no mode)
929       usage(stdout, help_mode);
930       fflush(NULL);
931       _Exit(0);
932     }
933   }
934 
935   // THEN: Scan for other binary-level actions (stops at mode name)
936   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
937     // Stop scanning at mode name - binary-level options must come before the mode
938     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
939       bool is_mode =
940           (strcmp(argv[i], "server") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "client") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "mirror") == 0 ||
941            strcmp(argv[i], "discovery") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "discovery-service") == 0);
942       if (is_mode) {
943         break; // Stop processing at mode name
944       }
945     }
946     if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
947       // Handle --quiet, -q, --quiet=value formats
948       bool temp_quiet = false;
949       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &temp_quiet, "quiet", 'q')) {
950         if (temp_quiet) {
951           user_quiet = true;
952         }
953       }
954       // Validate --log-level and --log-file require arguments
955       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-level") == 0) {
956         if (i + 1 >= argc || argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
957           log_error("--log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
958           return ERROR_USAGE;
959         }
960         i++; // Skip the argument in this loop
961       }
962       if (strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0) {
963         if (i + 1 >= argc || argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
964           log_error("%s requires a file path", argv[i]);
965           return ERROR_USAGE;
966         }
967         i++; // Skip the argument in this loop
968       }
969       // --help is handled in the first scan loop above (works before OR after mode)
970       // Parse --color early so it affects help output colors
971       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0) {
972         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
973           // Check if next arg is a mode name (not a color value)
974           const char *next_arg = argv[i + 1];
975           bool is_mode =
976               (strcmp(next_arg, "server") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "mirror") == 0 ||
977                strcmp(next_arg, "discovery") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "discovery-service") == 0);
978 
979           if (is_mode) {
980             // Next arg is a mode, not a color value - default to true
981             parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
982             color_setting_found = true;
983           } else {
984             // Try to parse as color value
985             char *error_msg = NULL;
986             if (parse_color_setting(next_arg, &parsed_color_setting, &error_msg)) {
987               color_setting_found = true;
988               i++; // Skip the setting argument
989             } else {
990               if (error_msg) {
991                 log_error("Error parsing --color: %s", error_msg);
992                 free(error_msg);
993               }
994             }
995           }
996         } else {
997           // --color without argument defaults to true (enable colors)
998           parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
999           color_setting_found = true;
1000         }
1001       }
1002       // Check for --color=value format
1003       if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
1004         const char *value = argv[i] + 8;
1005         char *error_msg = NULL;
1006         if (parse_color_setting(value, &parsed_color_setting, &error_msg)) {
1007           color_setting_found = true;
1008         } else {
1009           if (error_msg) {
1010             log_error("Error parsing --color: %s", error_msg);
1011             free(error_msg);
1012           }
1013         }
1014       }
1015       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--version") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-v") == 0) {
1016         show_version = true;
1017         has_action = true;
1018         break;
1019       }
1020       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--check-update") == 0) {
1021         check_update_flag_seen = true;
1022         if (no_check_update_flag_seen) {
1023           log_error("Cannot specify both --check-update and --no-check-update");
1024           return ERROR_USAGE;
1025         }
1026         has_action = true;
1027         action_check_update_immediate();
1028         // action_check_update_immediate() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1029         break;
1030       }
1031       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--no-check-update") == 0) {
1032         no_check_update_flag_seen = true;
1033         if (check_update_flag_seen) {
1034           log_error("Cannot specify both --check-update and --no-check-update");
1035           return ERROR_USAGE;
1036         }
1037         // Flag will be parsed normally later
1038       }
1039       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--config-create") == 0) {
1040         create_config = true;
1041         has_action = true;
1042         // Check for optional [FILE] argument
1043         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1044           config_create_path = argv[i + 1];
1045           i++; // Consume the file path argument
1046         }
1047         break;
1048       }
1049       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--man-page-create") == 0) {
1050         create_manpage = true;
1051         has_action = true;
1052         // Check for optional [FILE] argument
1053         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1054           manpage_create_path = argv[i + 1];
1055           i++; // Consume the file path argument
1056         }
1057         break;
1058       }
1059       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--completions") == 0) {
1060         has_action = true;
1061         // Handle --completions: generate shell completion scripts
1062         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1063           const char *shell_name = argv[i + 1];
1064           i++; // Consume shell name
1065 
1066           // Check for optional output file
1067           const char *output_file = NULL;
1068           if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1069             output_file = argv[i + 1];
1070             i++; // Consume output file
1071           }
1072 
1073           action_completions(shell_name, output_file);
1074           // action_completions() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1075         } else {
1076           log_error("--completions requires shell name (bash, fish, zsh, powershell)");
1077           return ERROR_USAGE;
1078         }
1079         break; // Unreachable, but for clarity
1080       }
1081       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-webcams") == 0) {
1082         has_action = true;
1083         action_list_webcams();
1084         // action_list_webcams() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1085         break;
1086       }
1087       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-microphones") == 0) {
1088         has_action = true;
1089         action_list_microphones();
1090         // action_list_microphones() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1091         break;
1092       }
1093       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-speakers") == 0) {
1094         has_action = true;
1095         action_list_speakers();
1096         // action_list_speakers() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1097         break;
1098       }
1099       // Check for --show-capabilities (binary-level action)
1100       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--show-capabilities") == 0) {
1101         has_action = true;
1102         action_show_capabilities_immediate();
1103         // action_show_capabilities_immediate() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1104         break;
1105       }
1106     }
1107   }
1108   // Store action flag globally for use during cleanup
1109   set_action_flag(has_action);
1110   // ========================================================================
1111   // STAGE 1B: DO MODE DETECTION EARLY (needed for log_init)
1112   // ========================================================================
1113   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY; // Default mode
1114   char detected_session_string[SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE] = {0};
1115   int mode_index = -1;
1116 
1117   asciichat_error_t mode_detect_result =
1118       options_detect_mode(argc, argv, &detected_mode, detected_session_string, &mode_index);
1119   if (mode_detect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1120     return mode_detect_result;
1121   }
1122 
1123   // VALIDATE: Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
1124   // Check if any binary-level options appear after the mode position
1125   if (mode_index > 0) {
1126     for (int i = mode_index + 1; i < argc; i++) {
1127       if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
1128         bool takes_arg = false;
1129         bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1130 
1131         if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1132           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Binary-level option '%s' must appear before the mode '%s', not after it",
1133                            argv[i], argv[mode_index]);
1134         }
1135       }
1136     }
1137   }
1138   // ========================================================================
1139   // STAGE 1C: Initialize logging EARLY (before any log_dev calls)
1140   // ========================================================================
1141   // Create local options struct and initialize with defaults
1142   options_t opts = options_t_new(); // Initialize with all defaults
1143   opts.detected_mode = detected_mode;
1144   char *log_filename = options_get_log_filepath(detected_mode, opts);
1145   SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.log_file, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", log_filename);
1146   // Note: log_init() is called earlier in asciichat_shared_init() and will be
1147   // reconfigured with the actual log level and file in main.c after options are fully parsed
1148   // NOTE: --color detection now happens in src/main.c BEFORE asciichat_shared_init()
1149   // This ensures g_color_flag_passed and g_color_flag_value are set before any logging.
1150   //
1151   // NOTE: Timer system and shared subsystems are initialized by src/main.c
1152   // via asciichat_shared_init() BEFORE options_init() is called.
1153   // This allows options_init() to use properly configured logging.
1154 
1155   // If an action flag is detected OR user passed --quiet, silence logs for clean output
1156   if (user_quiet || has_action) {
1157     log_set_terminal_output(false); // Suppress console logging for clean action output
1158   }
1159   // Apply parsed color setting from STAGE 1A
1160   if (color_setting_found) {
1161     opts.color = parsed_color_setting;
1162   } else {
1163     opts.color = OPT_COLOR_DEFAULT; // Apply default
1164   }
1165 
1166   // If we found version/config-create/create-manpage, handle them immediately (before mode detection)
1167   if (show_version || create_config || create_manpage) {
1168     if (show_version) {
1169       opts.version = true;
1170       options_state_set(&opts);
1171       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1172     } else if (create_config) {
1173       // Build the schema first so config_create_default can generate options from it
1174       const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1175       if (unified_config) {
1176         asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
1177         if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1178           // Schema build failed, but continue anyway
1179           (void)schema_build_result;
1180         }
1181         options_config_destroy(unified_config);
1182       }
1183 
1184       // Call action handler which handles all output and prompts properly
1185       action_create_config(config_create_path);
1186       // action_create_config() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1187     } else if (create_manpage) {
1188       // Call action handler which handles all output and prompts properly
1189       action_create_manpage(manpage_create_path);
1190       // action_create_manpage() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1191     }
1192   }
1193 
1194   // Mode detection and logging already initialized early in STAGE 1B/1C above
1195   // (moved earlier to ensure logging is available before any log_dev() calls)
1196 
1197   // Check for binary-level options that can appear before or after mode
1198   // Search entire argv to find --quiet, --log-file, --log-level, -V, etc.
1199   // These are documented as binary-level options that can appear anywhere
1200   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
1201     if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
1202       // Handle -V and --verbose (stackable verbosity)
1203       if (strcmp(argv[i], "-V") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--verbose") == 0) {
1204         // Check if next argument is a number (optional argument)
1205         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1206           // Try to parse as integer count
1207           char *endptr;
1208           long value = strtol(argv[i + 1], &endptr, 10);
1209           if (*endptr == '\0' && value >= 0 && value <= 100) {
1210             opts.verbose_level = (unsigned short int)value;
1211             i++; // Skip next argument
1212             continue;
1213           }
1214         }
1215         // No valid number, just increment
1216         opts.verbose_level++;
1217       }
1218       // Handle binary-level boolean options using the abstraction function
1219       // -q/--quiet, --json, --log-format-console all use the same parser
1220       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.quiet, "quiet", 'q')) {
1221         continue;
1222       }
1223       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.json, "json", '\0')) {
1224         continue;
1225       }
1226       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.log_format_console_only, "log-format-console", '\0')) {
1227         continue;
1228       }
1229       // Handle --log-level LEVEL (set log threshold)
1230       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-level") == 0) {
1231         if (i + 1 >= argc) {
1232           log_error("--log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
1233           return ERROR_USAGE;
1234         }
1235         if (argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
1236           log_error("--log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
1237           return ERROR_USAGE;
1238         }
1239         char *error_msg = NULL;
1240         if (parse_log_level(argv[i + 1], &opts.log_level, &error_msg)) {
1241           i++; // Skip the level argument
1242         } else {
1243           if (error_msg) {
1244             log_error("%s", error_msg);
1245             free(error_msg);
1246           } else {
1247             log_error("invalid log level value: %s", argv[i + 1]);
1248           }
1249           return ERROR_USAGE;
1250         }
1251       }
1252       // Handle -L and --log-file FILE (set log file path)
1253       if ((strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0)) {
1254         if (i + 1 >= argc) {
1255           log_error("%s requires a file path", argv[i]);
1256           return ERROR_USAGE;
1257         }
1258         if (argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
1259           log_error("%s requires a file path", argv[i]);
1260           return ERROR_USAGE;
1261         }
1262         SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.log_file, argv[i + 1], sizeof(opts.log_file));
1263         i++; // Skip the file argument
1264       }
1265       // NOTE: --color is now parsed in STAGE 1A (early, before help processing)
1266       // to ensure help output colors are applied correctly
1267     }
1268   }
1269 
1270   if (show_version) {
1271     // Show binary-level version from src/main.c
1272     opts.version = true;
1273     options_state_set(&opts);
1274     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1275   }
1276 
1277   if (create_config) {
1278     // Handle --config-create: create default config file and exit
1279     // Use provided path or default to user config location
1280     char config_path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1281     if (config_create_path) {
1282       SAFE_STRNCPY(config_path, config_create_path, sizeof(config_path));
1283     } else {
1284       // Use default config path: ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml
1285       char *config_dir = get_config_dir();
1286       if (!config_dir) {
1287         log_error("Error: Failed to determine default config directory");
1288         return ERROR_CONFIG;
1289       }
1290       safe_snprintf(config_path, sizeof(config_path), "%sconfig.toml", config_dir);
1291       SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1292     }
1293 
1294     // Create config with default options
1295     asciichat_error_t result = config_create_default(config_path);
1296     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1297       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1298       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1299         log_error("Error creating config: %s", err_ctx.context_message);
1300       } else {
1301         log_error("Error: Failed to create config file at %s", config_path);
1302       }
1303       return result;
1304     }
1305 
1306     log_plain("Created default config file at: %s", config_path);
1307     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return successfully after creating config
1308   }
1309 
1310   if (create_manpage) {
1311     // Handle --create-man-page-template: generate merged man page template
1312     // The .1.in file is the existing template to read from (not the output)
1313     const char *existing_template_path = "share/man/man1/ascii-chat.1.in";
1314     options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1315     if (!config) {
1316       log_error("Error: Failed to get binary options config");
1317       return ERROR_MEMORY;
1318     }
1319 
1320     // Generate merged man page from embedded or filesystem resources
1321     // (existing_template_path and manpage_content_file parameters are no longer supported)
1322     asciichat_error_t err = options_config_generate_manpage_merged(config, "ascii-chat", NULL, existing_template_path,
1323                                                                    "Video chat in your terminal");
1324 
1325     options_config_destroy(config);
1326 
1327     if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1328       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1329       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1330         log_error("%s", err_ctx.context_message);
1331       } else {
1332         log_error("Error: Failed to generate man page");
1333       }
1334       return err;
1335     }
1336 
1337     log_plain("Generated man page: %s", existing_template_path);
1338     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return successfully after generating man page
1339   }
1340 
1341   // ========================================================================
1342   // STAGE 2: Build argv for mode-specific parsing
1343   // ========================================================================
1344   // If mode was found, build argv with only arguments after the mode
1345   // If mode_index == -1, filter out binary-level options from all arguments
1346   int mode_argc = argc;
1347   char **mode_argv = (char **)argv;
1348   char **allocated_mode_argv = NULL; // Track if we need to free mode_argv
1349 
1350   if (mode_index == -1) {
1351     // No explicit mode - filter binary-level options from entire argv
1352     int max_mode_argc = argc; // Worst case: no binary opts skipped
1353 
1354     if (max_mode_argc > 256) {
1355       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Too many arguments: %d", max_mode_argc);
1356     }
1357 
1358     char **new_mode_argv = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)(max_mode_argc + 1) * sizeof(char *), char **);
1359     if (!new_mode_argv) {
1360       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mode_argv");
1361     }
1362     allocated_mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1363 
1364     new_mode_argv[0] = argv[0]; // Copy program name
1365 
1366     // Copy all args except binary-level options
1367     int new_argv_idx = 1;
1368     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
1369       bool takes_arg = false;
1370       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1371 
1372       // Check if this is a binary-level option
1373       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1374         // Skip argument if needed
1375         // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone)
1376         if (takes_arg && i + 1 < argc) {
1377           i++; // Skip required argument
1378         } else if (takes_optional_arg && i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1379           i++; // Skip optional argument
1380         }
1381         continue;
1382       }
1383       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1384       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx++] = argv[i];
1385     }
1386 
1387     mode_argc = new_argv_idx;
1388     new_mode_argv[mode_argc] = NULL;
1389     mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1390   } else if (mode_index != -1) {
1391     // Mode found at position mode_index
1392     // Build new argv: [program_name, args_before_mode (no binary opts)..., args_after_mode...]
1393     // Binary-level options are not passed to mode-specific parsers
1394 
1395     int args_after_mode = argc - mode_index - 1;
1396     // We'll calculate final argc after skipping binary options
1397     int max_mode_argc = 1 + (mode_index - 1) + args_after_mode; // Worst case: no binary opts skipped
1398 
1399     if (max_mode_argc > 256) {
1400       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Too many arguments: %d", max_mode_argc);
1401     }
1402 
1403     // Allocate max_mode_argc+1 to accommodate NULL terminator
1404     char **new_mode_argv = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)(max_mode_argc + 1) * sizeof(char *), char **);
1405     if (!new_mode_argv) {
1406       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mode_argv");
1407     }
1408     allocated_mode_argv = new_mode_argv; // Track allocation for cleanup
1409 
1410     // Copy: [program_name, args_before_mode (excluding binary opts)..., args_after_mode...]
1411     if (mode_index == 0) {
1412       // Mode is at argv[0], use "ascii-chat" as program name
1413       new_mode_argv[0] = "ascii-chat";
1414     } else {
1415       new_mode_argv[0] = argv[0];
1416     }
1417 
1418     // Copy args before mode, skipping binary-level options
1419     int new_argv_idx = 1;
1420     for (int i = 1; i < mode_index; i++) {
1421       bool takes_arg = false;
1422       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1423 
1424       // Check if this is a binary-level option
1425       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1426         // Skip argument if needed
1427         if ((takes_arg || (takes_optional_arg && argv[i + 1][0] != '-')) && i + 1 < mode_index) {
1428           i++; // Skip argument
1429         }
1430         continue;
1431       }
1432       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1433       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx++] = argv[i];
1434     }
1435     // Copy args after mode, filtering out any binary-level options (they shouldn't appear here)
1436     int args_after_mode_idx = 0;
1437     for (int i = mode_index + 1; i < argc; i++) {
1438       bool takes_arg = false;
1439       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1440 
1441       // Check if this is a binary-level option (shouldn't appear after mode)
1442       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1443         // Skip binary-level option and its argument if needed
1444         if ((takes_arg || (takes_optional_arg && argv[i + 1][0] != '-')) && i + 1 < argc) {
1445           i++; // Skip argument
1446         }
1447         continue; // Skip this option
1448       }
1449       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1450       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx + args_after_mode_idx++] = argv[i];
1451     }
1452     // Calculate actual argc (program + filtered args before + filtered args after)
1453     mode_argc = new_argv_idx + args_after_mode_idx;
1454     new_mode_argv[mode_argc] = NULL;
1455 
1456     mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1457   }
1458 
1459   // ========================================================================
1460   // STAGE 3: Set Mode-Specific Defaults
1461   // ========================================================================
1462   // Initialize all defaults using options_t_new_preserve_binary() to keep binary-level
1463   // options (--quiet, --verbose, --log-level, etc.) from being reset
1464   opts = options_t_new_preserve_binary(&opts);
1465   // If a session string was detected during mode detection, restore it after options reset
1466   // This ensures discovery mode knows which session to join
1467   if (detected_session_string[0] != '\0') {
1468     SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.session_string, detected_session_string, sizeof(opts.session_string));
1469     log_info("options_init: Detected session string from argv: '%s'", detected_session_string);
1470   }
1471 
1472   // Encryption options default to disabled/empty
1473   opts.no_encrypt = 0;
1474   opts.encrypt_key[0] = '\0';
1475   opts.password[0] = '\0';
1476   opts.encrypt_keyfile[0] = '\0';
1477   opts.server_key[0] = '\0';
1478   opts.client_keys[0] = '\0';
1479   opts.palette_custom[0] = '\0';
1480 
1481   // Set different default addresses for different modes
1482   if (detected_mode == MODE_CLIENT || detected_mode == MODE_MIRROR || detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY) {
1483     // Client/Mirror/Discovery: connects to localhost by default (discovery uses ACDS to find server)
1484     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "localhost");
1485     opts.address6[0] = '\0'; // Client doesn't use address6
1486   } else if (detected_mode == MODE_SERVER) {
1487     // Server: binds to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6) by default
1488     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "127.0.0.1");
1489     // address6 is now a positional argument, not an option
1490     opts.address6[0] = '\0';
1491   } else if (detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1492     // ACDS: binds to all interfaces by default
1493     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "0.0.0.0");
1494     opts.address6[0] = '\0';
1495   }
1496 
1497   // ========================================================================
1498   // STAGE 4: Build Dynamic Schema from Unified Options Config
1499   // ========================================================================
1500   // Build the config schema dynamically from the unified config
1501   // This generates TOML keys, CLI flags, categories, and types from builder data
1502   const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1503   if (unified_config) {
1504     asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
1505     if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1506       // Schema build failed, but continue with static schema as fallback
1507       (void)schema_build_result;
1508     } else {
1509     }
1510     options_config_destroy(unified_config);
1511   } else {
1512   }
1513 
1514   // ========================================================================
1515   // STAGE 5: Load Configuration Files
1516   // ========================================================================
1517   // Extract binary-level options BEFORE config loading (config may reset them)
1518   binary_level_opts_t binary_before_config = extract_binary_level(&opts);
1519   // Publish options to RCU before config loading so that config logs get proper colors
1520   // from the parsed --color setting (e.g., --color=true)
1521   asciichat_error_t config_publish_result = options_state_set(&opts);
1522   (void)config_publish_result; // Suppress unused variable warning
1523   // Load config files - now uses detected_mode directly for bitmask validation
1524   // Save flip_x and flip_y as they should not be reset by config files
1525   // Also save encryption settings - they should only be controlled via CLI, not config file
1526   bool saved_flip_x_from_config = opts.flip_x;
1527   bool saved_flip_y_from_config = opts.flip_y;
1528   bool saved_encrypt_enabled = opts.encrypt_enabled;
1529   asciichat_error_t config_result = config_load_system_and_user(detected_mode, false, &opts);
1530   (void)config_result; // Continue with defaults and CLI parsing regardless of result
1531   // Restore binary-level options (don't let config override command-line options)
1532   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary_before_config);
1533 
1534   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - config shouldn't override the defaults
1535   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x_from_config;
1536   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y_from_config;
1537 
1538   // Restore encrypt_enabled - it should only be set via CLI, not config file
1539   // The config can set key/password which auto-enables encryption, but the encrypt_enabled
1540   // flag itself should stay at its default unless the user explicitly passes --encrypt
1541   opts.encrypt_enabled = saved_encrypt_enabled;
1542 
1543   // ========================================================================
1544   // STAGE 6: Parse Command-Line Arguments (Unified)
1545   // ========================================================================
1546   // Extract binary-level options BEFORE applying unified defaults
1547   // (which might override them from config files)
1548   binary_level_opts_t binary_before_defaults = extract_binary_level(&opts);
1549   asciichat_mode_t mode_saved_for_parsing = detected_mode; // CRITICAL: Save before defaults reset
1550   // Get unified config
1551   options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1552   if (!config) {
1553     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1554     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to create options configuration");
1555   }
1556   int remaining_argc;
1557   char **remaining_argv;
1558 
1559   // Save flip_x and flip_y before applying defaults (should not be reset by defaults)
1560   bool saved_flip_x = opts.flip_x;
1561   bool saved_flip_y = opts.flip_y;
1562   // Apply defaults from unified config
1563   asciichat_error_t defaults_result = options_config_set_defaults(config, &opts);
1564   if (defaults_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1565     options_config_destroy(config);
1566     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1567     return defaults_result;
1568   }
1569   // Restore binary-level options (they should never be overridden by defaults)
1570   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary_before_defaults);
1571 
1572   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - they should keep the values from options_t_new()
1573   // unless explicitly set by the user (but defaults shouldn't override them)
1574   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x;
1575   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y;
1576 
1577   // Restore detected_mode before parsing so mode validation works.
1578   opts.detected_mode = mode_saved_for_parsing;
1579 
1580   // Save flip_x and flip_y before parsing - they should not be reset by the parser
1581   bool saved_flip_x_for_parse = opts.flip_x;
1582   bool saved_flip_y_for_parse = opts.flip_y;
1583   // Note: json is already saved via extract_binary_level/restore_binary_level mechanism
1584   // Parse mode-specific arguments
1585   option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bitmask = (1 << mode_saved_for_parsing);
1586   log_debug("About to parse options_config with mode_bitmask=%u, mode_argc=%d", mode_bitmask, mode_argc);
1587   asciichat_error_t result =
1588       options_config_parse(config, mode_argc, mode_argv, &opts, mode_bitmask, &remaining_argc, &remaining_argv);
1589   log_debug("options_config_parse returned: %d", result);
1590   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - they should keep their values unless explicitly overridden
1591   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x_for_parse;
1592   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y_for_parse;
1593   // json is already restored via the call to options_state_set which calls restore_binary_level
1594   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1595     options_config_destroy(config);
1596     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1597     // Convert ERROR_CONFIG to ERROR_USAGE for command-line parsing errors
1598     if (result == ERROR_CONFIG) {
1599       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1600       const char *error_detail = "invalid options";
1601       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1602         error_detail = err_ctx.context_message;
1603       }
1604       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Failed to parse options: %s", error_detail);
1605     }
1606     return result;
1607   }
1608 
1609   // ========================================================================
1610   // STAGE 6.5: Publish Parsed Options Early
1611   // ========================================================================
1612   // Publish options to RCU as soon as they're parsed
1613   // This ensures GET_OPTION() works during cleanup even if validation fails
1614   log_debug("Publishing parsed options to RCU before validation");
1615   asciichat_error_t early_publish = options_state_set(&opts);
1616   if (early_publish != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1617     log_error("Failed to publish parsed options to RCU state early");
1618     options_config_destroy(config);
1619     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1620     return early_publish;
1621   }
1622   log_debug("Successfully published options to RCU");
1623 
1624   // Auto-enable custom palette if palette-chars was set
1625   if (opts.palette_custom[0] != '\0') {
1626     // palette-chars was set - always use PALETTE_CUSTOM (overrides any explicit --palette setting)
1627     opts.palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
1628     opts.palette_custom_set = true;
1629     log_debug("Set PALETTE_CUSTOM because --palette-chars was provided");
1630 
1631     // Validate palette characters for UTF-8 correctness
1632     if (!utf8_is_valid(opts.palette_custom)) {
1633       log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains invalid UTF-8 sequences");
1634       options_config_destroy(config);
1635       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1636       return option_error_invalid();
1637     }
1638 
1639     // Check if palette contains non-ASCII characters
1640     bool has_non_ascii = !utf8_is_ascii_only(opts.palette_custom);
1641     if (has_non_ascii) {
1642       // Non-ASCII characters require UTF-8 support
1643       // Check if UTF-8 is explicitly disabled or unavailable
1644       bool utf8_disabled = (opts.force_utf8 == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE);
1645       bool utf8_auto_unavailable = (opts.force_utf8 == COLOR_SETTING_AUTO && !terminal_supports_utf8());
1646 
1647       if (utf8_disabled) {
1648         log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains non-ASCII characters but --utf8=false was specified");
1649         log_error("       Remove --utf8=false or use ASCII-only palette characters");
1650         options_config_destroy(config);
1651         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1652         return option_error_invalid();
1653       }
1654 
1655       if (utf8_auto_unavailable) {
1656         log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains non-ASCII characters but terminal does not support UTF-8");
1657         log_error("       Use --utf8=true to force UTF-8 mode or use ASCII-only palette characters");
1658         options_config_destroy(config);
1659         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1660         return option_error_invalid();
1661       }
1662     }
1663   }
1664 
1665   // Auto-enable encryption if key was provided
1666   if (opts.encrypt_key[0] != '\0') {
1667     // Validate key file exists (skip for remote/virtual keys)
1668     if (!is_remote_key_path(opts.encrypt_key)) {
1669       struct stat st;
1670       if (stat(opts.encrypt_key, &st) != 0) {
1671         log_error("Key file not found: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1672         options_config_destroy(config);
1673         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1674         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Key file not found: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1675       }
1676       if ((st.st_mode & S_IFMT) != S_IFREG) {
1677         log_error("Key path is not a regular file: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1678         options_config_destroy(config);
1679         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1680         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Key path is not a regular file: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1681       }
1682     }
1683     opts.encrypt_enabled = 1;
1684     log_debug("Auto-enabled encryption because --key was provided");
1685   }
1686 
1687   // Color filter validation and auto-enable
1688   if (opts.color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE) {
1689     // Color filter requires color to be enabled
1690     if (opts.color == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE) {
1691       log_error("Error: --color-filter cannot be used with --color=false");
1692       options_config_destroy(config);
1693       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1694       return option_error_invalid();
1695     }
1696     // Auto-enable color when color filter is specified
1697     opts.color = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
1698     log_debug("Auto-enabled color because --color-filter was provided");
1699   }
1700 
1701   // Detect if splash or status_screen were explicitly set on command line
1702   for (int i = 0; i < mode_argc; i++) {
1703     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1704         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen=", 16) == 0)) {
1705       // Parse the value if provided (--splash-screen=true/false)
1706       bool splash_value = true; // Default to true for plain --splash-screen
1707       if (strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen=", 16) == 0) {
1708         const char *value = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1709         if (strcasecmp(value, "false") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "no") == 0 ||
1710             strcasecmp(value, "0") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "off") == 0) {
1711           splash_value = false;
1712         }
1713       }
1714       opts.splash_screen = splash_value;
1715       opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set = true;
1716     }
1717     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1718         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen=", 16) == 0)) {
1719       // Parse the value if provided (--status-screen=true/false)
1720       bool status_value = true; // Default to true for plain --status-screen
1721       if (strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen=", 16) == 0) {
1722         const char *value = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1723         if (strcasecmp(value, "false") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "no") == 0 ||
1724             strcasecmp(value, "0") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "off") == 0) {
1725           status_value = false;
1726         }
1727       }
1728       opts.status_screen = status_value;
1729       opts.status_screen_explicitly_set = true;
1730     }
1731 #ifndef NDEBUG
1732     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1733         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state=", 13) == 0)) {
1734       opts.debug_sync_state_time_explicit = true;
1735       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state") == 0) {
1736         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1737           char *endptr;
1738           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1739           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1740             opts.debug_sync_state_time = val;
1741             i++;
1742           }
1743         }
1744       } else {
1745         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 13;
1746         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1747           char *endptr;
1748           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1749           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1750             opts.debug_sync_state_time = val;
1751           }
1752         }
1753       }
1754     }
1755     if (mode_argv[i] && (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace=", 12) == 0)) {
1756       opts.debug_backtrace_time_explicit = true;
1757       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
1758         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1759           char *endptr;
1760           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1761           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1762             opts.debug_backtrace_time = val;
1763             i++;
1764           }
1765         }
1766       } else {
1767         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 12;
1768         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1769           char *endptr;
1770           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1771           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1772             opts.debug_backtrace_time = val;
1773           }
1774         }
1775       }
1776     }
1777     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1778         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report=", 16) == 0)) {
1779       opts.debug_memory_report_interval_explicit = true;
1780       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report") == 0) {
1781         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1782           char *endptr;
1783           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1784           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1785             opts.debug_memory_report_interval = val;
1786             i++;
1787           }
1788         }
1789       } else {
1790         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1791         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1792           char *endptr;
1793           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1794           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1795             opts.debug_memory_report_interval = val;
1796           }
1797         }
1798       }
1799     }
1800 #endif
1801   }
1802 
1803   // Auto-disable splash when grep is used (since it's one-time startup screen)
1804   // UNLESS it was explicitly set by the user
1805   // Status screen is now compatible with --grep since we support auto-loading patterns
1806   bool grep_was_provided = false;
1807   for (int i = 0; i < mode_argc; i++) {
1808     if (mode_argv[i] && (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--grep") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--grep=", 7) == 0)) {
1809       grep_was_provided = true;
1810       break;
1811     }
1812   }
1813 
1814   if (grep_was_provided) {
1815     if (!opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set) {
1816       opts.splash_screen = false;
1817       log_debug("Auto-disabled splash because --grep was provided");
1818     }
1819   }
1820 
1821   // Auto-disable splash and status screens when terminal is non-interactive or CLAUDECODE is set
1822   // This ensures clean output when running under LLM automation or non-interactive shells
1823   bool is_terminal_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1824   bool is_claudecode_set = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
1825   bool should_auto_disable_screens = !is_terminal_interactive || is_claudecode_set;
1826 
1827   if (should_auto_disable_screens) {
1828     if (!opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set) {
1829       opts.splash_screen = false;
1830       log_debug("Auto-disabled splash (non-interactive terminal or CLAUDECODE set)");
1831     }
1832     if (!opts.status_screen_explicitly_set) {
1833       opts.status_screen = false;
1834       log_debug("Auto-disabled status screen (non-interactive terminal or CLAUDECODE set)");
1835     }
1836   }
1837 
1838   // Validate all string options contain valid UTF-8
1839   // This prevents crashes and corruption from invalid UTF-8 in any option
1840   // DISABLED IN RELEASE BUILDS: utf8_is_valid() has performance issues with musl
1841   #ifndef NDEBUG
1842   const char *string_fields[][2] = {{"address", opts.address},
1843                                     {"address6", opts.address6},
1844                                     {"encrypt_key", opts.encrypt_key},
1845                                     {"encrypt_keyfile", opts.encrypt_keyfile},
1846                                     {"server_key", opts.server_key},
1847                                     {"client_keys", opts.client_keys},
1848                                     {"discovery_server", opts.discovery_server},
1849                                     {"discovery_service_key", opts.discovery_service_key},
1850                                     {"discovery_database_path", opts.discovery_database_path},
1851                                     {"log_file", opts.log_file},
1852                                     {"media_file", opts.media_file},
1853                                     {"palette_custom", opts.palette_custom},
1854                                     {"stun_servers", opts.stun_servers},
1855                                     {"turn_servers", opts.turn_servers},
1856                                     {"turn_username", opts.turn_username},
1857                                     {"turn_credential", opts.turn_credential},
1858                                     {"turn_secret", opts.turn_secret},
1859                                     {"session_string", opts.session_string},
1860                                     {NULL, NULL}};
1861 
1862   for (int i = 0; string_fields[i][0] != NULL; i++) {
1863     const char *field_name = string_fields[i][0];
1864     const char *field_value = string_fields[i][1];
1865 
1866     // Skip empty strings
1867     if (!field_value || field_value[0] == '\0') {
1868       continue;
1869     }
1870 
1871     // Validate UTF-8
1872     if (!utf8_is_valid(field_value)) {
1873       log_error("Error: Option --%s contains invalid UTF-8 sequences", field_name);
1874       log_error("       Value: %s", field_value);
1875       options_config_destroy(config);
1876       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1877       return option_error_invalid();
1878     }
1879   }
1880   #endif
1881   // Validate options
1882   log_info("★ VALIDATE_OPTIONS_AND_REPORT: About to call");
1883   result = validate_options_and_report(config, &opts);
1884   log_info("★ VALIDATE_OPTIONS_AND_REPORT: Returned with result=%d", result);
1885   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1886     options_config_destroy(config);
1887     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1888     return result;
1889   }
1890   // Check for unexpected remaining arguments
1891   if (remaining_argc > 0) {
1892     log_error("Error: Unexpected arguments after options:");
1893     for (int i = 0; i < remaining_argc; i++) {
1894       log_error("  %s", remaining_argv[i]);
1895     }
1896     options_config_destroy(config);
1897     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1898     return option_error_invalid();
1899   }
1900 
1901   // Mode-specific post-processing
1902   // Apply mode-specific defaults (port, websocket-port)
1903   log_info("★ APPLY_MODE_SPECIFIC: About to call");
1904   apply_mode_specific_defaults(&opts);
1905   log_info("★ APPLY_MODE_SPECIFIC: Done");
1906   log_dev("Applied mode-specific defaults: port=%d, websocket_port=%d", opts.port, opts.websocket_port);
1907 
1908   if (detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1909     // Set default paths if not specified
1910     log_info("★ DISCOVERY_SERVICE: Handling discovery service defaults");
1911     if (opts.discovery_database_path[0] == '\0') {
1912       // Database: Try system-wide location first, fall back to user directories
1913       // Preference: /usr/local/var/ascii-chat/ > ~/.local/share/ascii-chat/ > ~/.config/ascii-chat/
1914       log_info("★ DISCOVERY_SERVICE: About to call get_discovery_database_dir()");
1915       char *db_dir = get_discovery_database_dir();
1916       log_info("★ DISCOVERY_SERVICE: get_discovery_database_dir() returned %p", (void*)db_dir);
1917       if (!db_dir) {
1918         options_config_destroy(config);
1919         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1920         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to get database directory (tried system and user locations)");
1921       }
1922       safe_snprintf(opts.discovery_database_path, sizeof(opts.discovery_database_path), "%sdiscovery.db", db_dir);
1923       SAFE_FREE(db_dir);
1924     }
1925   }
1926 
1927   log_info("★ OPTIONS_CONFIG_DESTROY: About to call");
1928   options_config_destroy(config);
1929   log_info("★ OPTIONS_CONFIG_DESTROY: Done");
1930 
1931   // ========================================================================
1932   // STAGE 7: Post-Processing & Validation
1933   // ========================================================================
1934 
1935   log_info("★ STAGE 7: Starting post-processing");
1936 
1937   // Collect multiple --key flags for multi-key support (server/ACDS only)
1938   // This enables servers to load both SSH and GPG keys and select the right one
1939   // during handshake based on what the client expects
1940   if (detected_mode == MODE_SERVER || detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1941     log_info("★ STAGE 7.1: About to collect identity keys");
1942     int num_keys = options_collect_identity_keys(&opts, argc, argv);
1943     log_info("★ STAGE 7.1: options_collect_identity_keys returned %d", num_keys);
1944     if (num_keys < 0) {
1945       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1946       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Failed to collect identity keys");
1947     }
1948     // num_keys == 0 is OK (no --key flags provided)
1949   }
1950 
1951   log_info("★ STAGE 7.2: About to update dimensions");
1952 
1953   // After parsing command line options, update dimensions
1954   // First set any auto dimensions to terminal size, then apply full height logic
1955 
1956   // Clear auto flags if dimensions were explicitly parsed from options
1957   // This must happen before update_dimensions_to_terminal_size to prevent auto-detected
1958   // values from overwriting user-specified dimensions
1959   // Note: width=0 and height=0 are special values meaning "auto-detect", so don't clear auto flags for those
1960   if (opts.width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT && opts.width != 0) {
1961     opts.auto_width = false;
1962   }
1963   if (opts.height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT && opts.height != 0) {
1964     opts.auto_height = false;
1965   }
1966   update_dimensions_to_terminal_size(&opts);
1967   js_log_options("BEFORE_UPDATE_DIMS");
1968   js_log_options("CALLING_UPDATE_DIM");
1969   update_dimensions_for_full_height(&opts);
1970   js_log_options("AFTER_UPDATE_DIM");
1971 
1972   // SKIP: verbose level adjustment - conflicts with raylib log level system
1973   // TODO: Properly separate our log levels from raylib's
1974   // if (opts.verbose_level > 0) {
1975   //   log_level_t current_level = log_get_level();
1976   //   int new_level = (int)current_level - (int)opts.verbose_level;
1977   //   if (new_level < LOG_DEV) {
1978   //     new_level = LOG_DEV;
1979   //   }
1980   //   log_set_level((log_level_t)new_level);
1981   // }
1982 
1983   js_log_options("L11");
1984   js_log_options("L12");
1985   js_log_options("L13");
1986   js_log_options("L14");
1987   js_log_options("L15");
1988   js_log_options("CHECKPOINT B");
1989 
1990   // SKIP: Check WEBCAM_DISABLED environment variable to enable test pattern mode
1991   // TODO: Debug why logging here causes hang
1992   // const char *webcam_disabled = SAFE_GETENV("WEBCAM_DISABLED");
1993   // if (webcam_disabled &&
1994   //     (strcmp(webcam_disabled, "1") == 0 || platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "true") == 0 ||
1995   //      platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "yes") == 0 || platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "on") == 0)) {
1996   //   opts.test_pattern = true;
1997   // }
1998 
1999   js_log_options("CHECKPOINT C");
2000 
2001   // Apply --no-compress interaction with audio encoding
2002   js_log_options("Before --no-compress check");
2003   if (opts.no_compress) {
2004     js_log_options("In no_compress block");
2005     opts.encode_audio = false;
2006     js_log_options("Set encode_audio to false");
2007     log_debug("--no-compress set: disabling audio encoding");
2008     js_log_options("After log_debug in no_compress");
2009   }
2010   js_log_options("After --no-compress check");
2011 
2012   // Set media_from_stdin flag if media_file is "-"
2013   js_log_options("Before media_from_stdin check");
2014   if (opts.media_file[0] != '\0' && strcmp(opts.media_file, "-") == 0) {
2015     js_log_options("In media_from_stdin block");
2016     opts.media_from_stdin = true;
2017     js_log_options("Set media_from_stdin to true");
2018     log_debug("Media file set to stdin");
2019     js_log_options("After log_debug in media_from_stdin");
2020   }
2021   js_log_options("After media_from_stdin check");
2022 
2023   // Validate --seek option
2024   js_log_options("Before --seek validation");
2025   js_log_options("Checking media_seek_timestamp");
2026   if (opts.media_seek_timestamp > 0.0) {
2027     js_log_options("In seek validation block");
2028     // Can't seek stdin
2029     if (opts.media_from_stdin) {
2030       js_log_options("Seek error: media_from_stdin");
2031       log_error("--seek cannot be used with stdin (--file -)");
2032       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2033       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2034     }
2035 
2036     // Require --file or --url
2037     js_log_options("Checking media_file and media_url for seek");
2038     if (opts.media_file[0] == '\0' && opts.media_url[0] == '\0') {
2039       js_log_options("Seek error: no media_file or media_url");
2040       log_error("--seek requires --file or --url");
2041       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2042       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2043     }
2044     js_log_options("Seek validation passed");
2045   }
2046   js_log_options("After --seek validation");
2047 
2048   // Validate --pause option
2049   js_log_options("Before --pause validation");
2050   js_log_options("Checking pause flag");
2051   if (opts.pause) {
2052     js_log_options("In pause validation block");
2053     // Require --file or --url (not webcam, not test pattern)
2054     if (opts.media_file[0] == '\0' && opts.media_url[0] == '\0') {
2055       js_log_options("Pause error: no media_file or media_url");
2056       log_error("--pause requires --file or --url");
2057       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2058       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2059     }
2060     js_log_options("Pause validation passed");
2061   }
2062   js_log_options("After --pause validation");
2063 
2064   js_log_options("Before media_url check");
2065   js_log_options("Checking media_url[0]");
2066   if (opts.media_url[0] != '\0') {
2067     js_log_options("Entering media_url block");
2068     // URL must be a valid HTTP(S) URL (YouTube URLs are HTTPS URLs)
2069     js_log_options("About to call url_is_valid");
2070     js_log_options("Before url_is_valid call");
2071     if (!url_is_valid(opts.media_url)) {
2072       js_log_options("url_is_valid returned false");
2073       log_error("--url must be a valid HTTP(S) URL: %s", opts.media_url);
2074       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2075       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2076     }
2077     js_log_options("url_is_valid returned true");
2078     js_log_options("url_is_valid returned, before strstr check");
2079 
2080     // Normalize bare URLs by prepending http:// if not present
2081     js_log_options("Before strstr check");
2082     if (!strstr(opts.media_url, "://")) {
2083       js_log_options("URL needs normalization");
2084       char normalized_url[2048];
2085       js_log_options("Before snprintf");
2086       int result = snprintf(normalized_url, sizeof(normalized_url), "http://%s", opts.media_url);
2087       js_log_options("After snprintf");
2088       if (result > 0 && result < (int)sizeof(normalized_url)) {
2089         js_log_options("snprintf successful, before SAFE_STRNCPY");
2090         SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.media_url, normalized_url, sizeof(opts.media_url));
2091         js_log_options("URL normalized");
2092       } else {
2093         js_log_options("snprintf failed");
2094         log_error("Failed to normalize URL (too long): %s", opts.media_url);
2095         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2096         return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2097       }
2098     }
2099     js_log_options("After normalization check");
2100   }
2101   js_log_options("After media_url block");
2102 
2103   // ========================================================================
2104   // STAGE 8: Publish to RCU
2105   // ========================================================================
2106 
2107   // Adjust flip_x default: if --file or --url is provided, default flip_x to false
2108   // (file/URL content is typically not horizontally flipped, unlike webcam)
2109   js_log_options("Before flip_x check");
2110   if ((opts.media_file[0] != '\0' || opts.media_url[0] != '\0') && opts.flip_x) {
2111     js_log_options("In flip_x adjustment");
2112     opts.flip_x = false;
2113     js_log_options("flip_x set to false");
2114     log_debug("flip_x auto-adjusted to false for media file/URL");
2115     js_log_options("After log_debug for flip_x");
2116   }
2117   js_log_options("After flip_x check");
2118 
2119   // Save the quiet flag before publishing (RCU will be cleaned up before memory report runs)
2120 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
2121   js_log_options("Before quiet_for_memory_report assignment");
2122   bool quiet_for_memory_report = opts.quiet;
2123   js_log_options("After quiet_for_memory_report assignment");
2124 #endif
2125 
2126   // Publish parsed options to RCU state (replaces options_state_populate_from_globals)
2127   // This makes the options visible to all threads via lock-free reads
2128   js_log_options("STAGE 8 RCU: Before options_state_set");
2129   js_log_options("Before log_info PERF");
2130   js_log_options("After log_info PERF");
2131   js_log_options("Calling options_state_set...");
2132   js_log_options("Before options_state_set call");
2133   asciichat_error_t publish_result = options_state_set(&opts);
2134   js_log_options("After options_state_set call");
2135   js_log_options("options_state_set returned");
2136   js_log_options("Before log_info options_state_set done");
2137   js_log_options("After log_info options_state_set done");
2138   js_log_options("Before publish_result check");
2139   if (publish_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2140     js_log_options("publish_result not OK");
2141     log_error("Failed to publish parsed options to RCU state: %d", publish_result);
2142     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2143     return publish_result;
2144   }
2145   js_log_options("publish_result is OK");
2146   js_log_options("options_state_set successful");
2147 
2148   // Now update debug memory quiet mode with the saved quiet value
2149 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
2150   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(quiet_for_memory_report);
2151 #endif
2152 
2153   // ========================================================================
2154   // Apply color scheme to logging
2155   // ========================================================================
2156   // Now that options are parsed, set and apply the selected color scheme to logging
2157   js_log_options("STAGE 8 ColorScheme: Checking color_scheme_name");
2158   js_log_options("Before color_scheme_name[0] check");
2159   if (opts.color_scheme_name[0] != '\0') {
2160     js_log_options("Color scheme name is set");
2161     js_log_options("Before colorscheme_set_active_scheme");
2162     js_log_options("Before colorscheme_set_active_scheme call");
2163     asciichat_error_t scheme_result = colorscheme_set_active_scheme(opts.color_scheme_name);
2164     js_log_options("After colorscheme_set_active_scheme call");
2165     js_log_options("colorscheme_set_active_scheme returned");
2166     js_log_options("Before log_info colorscheme done");
2167     js_log_options("After log_info colorscheme done");
2168     js_log_options("Before scheme_result check");
2169     if (scheme_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2170       js_log_options("scheme_result is OK");
2171       js_log_options("Before colorscheme_get_active_scheme");
2172       const color_scheme_t *scheme = colorscheme_get_active_scheme();
2173       js_log_options("After colorscheme_get_active_scheme");
2174       js_log_options("Before scheme null check");
2175       if (scheme) {
2176         js_log_options("scheme is not null");
2177         js_log_options("Before log_set_color_scheme");
2178         log_set_color_scheme(scheme);
2179         js_log_options("After log_set_color_scheme");
2180         js_log_options("Color scheme applied");
2181         js_log_options("Before log_debug color scheme");
2182         log_debug("Color scheme applied: %s", opts.color_scheme_name);
2183         js_log_options("After log_debug color scheme");
2184       } else {
2185         js_log_options("scheme is null");
2186         js_log_options("No scheme returned from colorscheme_get_active_scheme");
2187       }
2188     } else {
2189       log_warn("Failed to apply color scheme: %s", opts.color_scheme_name);
2190     }
2191   }
2192 
2193   // ========================================================================
2194   // STAGE 8: Execute deferred actions (after all options parsed and published)
2195   // ========================================================================
2196   // Deferred actions (--list-webcams, --list-microphones, --list-speakers, --show-capabilities)
2197   // are executed here after all options are fully parsed and published via RCU.
2198   // This ensures action output reflects the final parsed state (e.g., final dimensions
2199   // for --show-capabilities).
2200   // Re-enable terminal output so deferred actions can print their results.
2201   // It was disabled earlier (STAGE 1C) to suppress log noise during option parsing.
2202   if (has_action) {
2203     log_set_terminal_output(true);
2204   }
2205   actions_execute_deferred();
2206   SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2207   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2208 }

asciichat_error_t options_set_int (const char *
  field_name, int value)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/options/options.h>


Check if an option was explicitly set via command-line.
    Convenience macro for checking if a specific option was explicitly provided
    by the user via command-line arguments (as opposed to using a default
    value).




Usage Examples:




// Check if user explicitly provided --width
if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT("width")) {


    printf("User set width explicitly\n");
}
// Use in conditional logic
if (!IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT("color") && terminal_is_dark_mode()) {


    // Color not set, apply dark mode defaults
}



Parameters


option_name The long name of the option (e.g.,
  "width", "port", "color")


Returns


true if option was explicitly set, false otherwise


Note


Option name must be a string literal matching the
  long_name from registry



Case-sensitive: must match exact option name




No locks needed - lock-free read via atomic pointer




Set a single option field (thread-safe, RCU-based)




Convenience function for updating a single field in the options
    struct. Uses RCU (copy-on-write) internally for thread-safe updates.




Example:




options_set_int("width", 120);
options_set_int("port", 8080);
options_set_bool("audio_enabled", true);



Thread Safety: Multiple writers are serialized with a
    mutex. Readers are never blocked (lock-free reads via GET_OPTION).
